MCP remember found related prior learnings about runtime parity gates, MCP HTTP, and silent fallback drift.
One root learning survived SIP: transport parity is not product contract parity.
One fresh collapse artifact was written.
Path-scoped commits were created after the artifact ownership list was used as the staging source.

session_arc.where_we_were: We entered with Bravetto, AbëONE, voice-service, and Consciousness MCP partially overlapped: identity, witness, Railway, MCP HTTP, and customer-facing product wires were being discussed as if one missing variable could collapse them.
session_arc.what_happened: We separated projections from substrate. The session normalized Abë pronunciation away from Aybee toward A.B., removed silent fallback assumptions in witness/config flows, documented projection-specific env ownership, and then corrected the architecture language: AbëONE is the organism/substrate; consciousness-core is a core organ/runtime; MCP HTTP and stdio are transport lenses; product REST is a separate contract.
session_arc.what_it_means: The next move is no longer to add more variables to Railway. The move is to sync handlers: one core behavior, multiple adapters. If Consciousness HTTP is to replace Railway-owned voice behavior, it must expose the same product REST contracts currently owned by voice-service.
session_arc.what_we_chose_not_to_do: We did not claim production Railway env state without reading it. We did not collapse MCP tool parity into product endpoint parity. We did not broad-stage unrelated dirty files.

root.statement: Transport parity is not product contract parity.
prevents: Mistaking HTTP MCP /mcp parity with stdio for voice-service replacement readiness.
Removing provider secrets from Railway before Consciousness HTTP owns product-grade /speak and /transcribe contracts.
Duplicating voice logic across Railway and consciousness-core.
Treating customer MCP access as access to the whole AbëONE substrate.
Adding more fallback variables instead of projection-specific contracts.
Shipping local operator atoms such as afplay-based speak as browser/Twilio audio endpoints.
Debugging transport drift when the real gap is REST adapter coverage.

leaf: Pronunciation hint removal is complete enough as direct product/copy hygiene; it does not need a new universal invariant beyond existing Abë brand identity rules.
Railway variable uncertainty is an operational check, not a new root: verify via approved dashboard or redacted variable-name listing.

bugs_gaps_deltas.description: Consciousness HTTP and stdio share MCP tool registration through the same organism runtime, but Consciousness HTTP does not yet expose product-compatible voice REST routes equivalent to voice-service.
bugs_gaps_deltas.next_action: Add a route manifest and REST wrappers in consciousness-core for product voice contracts before switching Railway to delegate.

bugs_gaps_deltas.description: The existing consciousness speak atom is local/operator-oriented and uses local playback behavior; it is not a product audio stream contract.
bugs_gaps_deltas.next_action: Wrap the underlying speak library for HTTP audio output instead of calling the atom directly from products.

bugs_gaps_deltas.description: Several repos contain concurrent dirty files unrelated to this collapse window.
bugs_gaps_deltas.next_action: Before committing, stage only exact paths proven by diff to belong to this window.

manifest_coherence.next_action: Declare which product REST contracts Consciousness HTTP owns versus which MCP tools it exposes.
When REST wrappers exist, update voice-service docs so Railway is a thin transport edge rather than provider/runtime owner.

dod.expected_result: The next window can point to one handler source for each migrated product voice contract and prove HTTP MCP/stdio/product REST are adapters, not divergent implementations.
